name: Florida Key Lime Pie
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Filling:
5 large egg yolks
Lime juice (freshly squeezed): 1/2 cup
Sugar: 1/3 cup
Salt: 1/8 teaspoon
Lime peel (coarsely grated): 2 tablespoons
Heavy cream: 2 1/2 cups
Small lime (sliced, optional)
Crust Preparation:
Heat oven to 375 degrees F. In a 9-inch pie plate, combine graham cracker crumbs and 2 tablespoons sugar; stir in butter until blended. Press mixture firmly and evenly over bottom and sides of pie plate. Bake for 10 minutes until browned. Cool completely on a wire rack.
Filling Preparation:
In the top of a double boiler using a wire whisk, beat egg yolks, lime juice, 1/3 cup sugar, and salt until well blended. Set over simmering water; cook about 5 minutes, stirring constantly until the mixture is thick enough to coat the back of a metal spoon. Remove from heat and stir in 2 tablespoons grated lime peel. Refrigerate the mixture for about 45 minutes until cool.
Cream Preparation:
In a large bowl with an electric mixer at medium speed, beat cream until soft peaks form. Set aside 1 cup for garnish. Fold the cooled lime mixture into the remaining cream.
Assembly:
Spoon the filling into the prepared crust and refrigerate for at least 2 hours until set. To serve, spoon the remaining whipped cream decoratively over top of the pie. Sprinkle with the remaining 2 tablespoons grated lime peel. Garnish with lime slices if desired. Makes 8 servings.
